title: chylismia confertiflora
intro: Chylismia confertiflora, also known as the desert marigold, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable herb for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Chylismia confertiflora include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antihistamine to alleviate allergies. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, or salves, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
